title: Leaning Wall Ladder Desk
summary: A leaning ladder desk — a writing surface plus graduated shelves that rest against the wall on 5°-raked legs. Small footprint, no wall anchoring needed to stand.
description:
A leaning desk that props against the wall like a ladder: a work surface at desk height with open shelves stepping up above it, all carried on legs raked back at 5°. It takes almost no floor space and needs no assembly into the wall to stand — though a wall cleat is smart once it's loaded.

Build the shelf boxes first — attach the backs, then the sides — with 1-1/4" nails or staples and glue. Then the trick that makes a leaning piece sit flat: cut the top back corner off each leg, leaving a 2-1/2" flat that's square to the top of the leg, and attach the back support to that flat so the top of the desk meets the wall cleanly.

The last step is a leaning-furniture quirk worth understanding. Once you stand the desk in its final position against the wall, the bottom rail gets set level with the floor — which means it will NOT be square to the raked legs. That's correct: the legs lean, the floor is level, so the rail follows the floor, not the legs. Screw it in place with the desk standing where it'll live.

tools.note: The 5° leg cuts and the leg-top corner cut.

cutList.note: Both ends 5°, parallel, long point to short point.

steps.title: Build the shelves
steps.body: Build all the shelf boxes first. Attach the backs, then the sides, with 1-1/4" nails or staples and wood glue. The shelves step from deep at the bottom to shallow at the top to suit the lean.

steps.title: Cut the leg tops
steps.body: Cut the top back corner off each leg, leaving a 2-1/2" flat that is square to the top of the leg. This flat is what the back support lands on so the desk meets the wall cleanly despite the lean.

steps.title: Attach the back support
steps.body: Attach the back support (top rail) to the tops of the legs, matching the square cut from the previous step. Then attach the shelves to the legs at their heights.

steps.title: Set the bottom rail in place
steps.body: Lean the desk against the wall in its final position. Place the remaining bottom rail level with the floor — it will NOT be square to the raked legs, and that's correct: the legs lean, so the rail follows the floor, not the legs. Screw it in place, then sand and finish.

images.alt: Leaning ladder desk with a writing surface and graduated shelves against a wall
